mechanism: THC is a partial CB1/CB2 agonist and CBD modulates endocannabinoid signaling with additional receptor-level effects; clinical effects vary by THC: CBD ratio and route.
contraindications: avoid in psychotic disorders, severe anxiety, or unsupervised psychoactive use
safetyNotes: Common adverse effects include dizziness, cognitive slowing, anxiety, and dry mouth; high-THC products can precipitate panic or psychosis in vulnerable individuals. Avoid use during pregnancy and adolescence when possible.

sources.title: The health effects of cannabis and cannabinoids (National Academies, 2017)
sources.note: Comprehensive evidence review of efficacy and harms.

interactions: Avoid with: Additive sedation and psychomotor impairment with alcohol
May potentiate: THC/CBD can interact with CYP3A4 and CYP2C9 substrates/inhibitors
Caution with: potentially changing drug exposure
May potentiate: May increase tachycardia risk with sympathomimetic agents

traditional ceremonial or ritual context use
sideEffects: nausea, confusion, or anxiety with higher intake
allergic rash or itching in sensitive individuals
